user_outcome: The parallel loop resolves every role-and-model combination to a driver at startup and exits with a named, actionable error before any worker spawns — a missing driver or capability can never crash or strand a run mid-flight; workers dispatch only through the driver-backed RunSlice.
rationale: Finishes the nil-factory story at the parallel entry point. Grounding: cmd/sworn/run.go:134 builds runSliceFn WITHOUT setting NewAgent/NewVerifier, historically relying on RunSlice's nil-defaults (the documented SIGSEGV class); worker.go dispatches only through the opaque RunSliceFn (worker.go:96,302,359,444); the production factory helpers newAgentFromModel/newVerifierFromModel (run.go:343,367) gate CapChat at construction time — mid-run. After S06 the factories are gone; this slice adds the startup resolution sweep: before RunParallel spawns workers, resolve implementer/verifier/captain models plus every escalation-list entry through the registry and fail fast on any miss. Also aligns internal/run/parallel.go's dispatch wiring with the registry (its board-oracle reading was fixed in the render-drift release and is NOT touched here).
in_scope: Startup resolution sweep: implementer/verifier/captain + every escalation-list entry resolved before worker spawn
Mid-run driver-unavailability surfacing through the existing retry/escalation policy (track-local, no cascade)
out_of_scope: Any new retry/escalation policy design — the existing policy is consumed as-is
Board-oracle reading in parallel.go — fixed in the render-drift release, untouched here

effort_complexity.rationale: Startup sweep + deleting the retired factory helpers; the heavy rewiring already happened in S06.

risks.description: Startup resolution is too strict — it rejects a run whose escalation list names a model the user never reaches.
risks.mitigation: Deliberate and accepted: an unreachable escalation entry is a config defect surfaced at t=0 (fail-closed beats a 20-minute-in crash); the error names exactly which entry failed and what is registered.

risks.description: AC-03's 'auth expired... through the existing retry/escalation policy' assumes that policy still distinguishes terminal (auth) failures from transient ones after the driver rewire. This slice explicitly treats retry/escalation policy design as out-of-scope ('the existing policy is consumed as-is'), so the terminal/non-terminal distinction must already be correct by the time this slice lands.
risks.mitigation: Owned by S06-loop-dispatch-rewire R-03: S06 must make Result.ErrKind=="auth" halt fail-fast (mirroring today's model.IsTerminal/KindAuth at internal/run/slice.go:487) before this slice's AC-03 test can pass meaningfully. Sequencing dependency, not new work for this slice — noted here so 'consumed as-is' isn't read as 'nothing to verify.'

acceptance_criteria.text: When `sworn loop --release <r> --parallel` starts, the engine SHALL resolve the implementer, verifier, and captain models AND every escalation-list entry through Registry.Resolve for their roles BEFORE spawning any worker; on any failure it SHALL exit non-zero with an error naming the model, the role, and the registered prefixes/roles that ARE available (TestParallelStartupFailFast).

The retired factory helpers newAgentFromModel and newVerifierFromModel in cmd/sworn/run.go SHALL be deleted, and no code path in cmd/sworn or internal/scheduler SHALL construct an agent or verifier outside the registry (asserted by the S06 import-boundary test extended to cmd/sworn's loop wiring).

If a driver becomes unavailable mid-run (CLI binary removed, auth expired), the dispatch SHALL surface Status=error with its ErrKind through the existing retry/escalation policy for that slice only — sibling tracks continue (no phase-wide cascade cancel), proven by a scheduler test with a fake driver that fails after N dispatches.
